title: Phase E: Release artifacts (version bump 2.0.0-rc.1 + CHANGELOG + README banner + git tag + GitHub prerelease)
scope: package.json + CHANGELOG.md + README.md + git tag v2.0.0-rc.1 + GitHub prerelease
description: Final release artifacts commit + tag + GitHub release. (1) `package.json` 2.0.0-rc.1 (single commit, retroactive on rc.1 commit). (2) CHANGELOG entry: BREAKING + Removed (v1.x list) + Added (v2 list) + Coming next (rc.2/3/4 preview). (3) Annotated git tag `v2.0.0-rc.1`. (4) GitHub release marked `prerelease: true`; release notes = CHANGELOG entry. (5) **NOT publish to npm** during rc.x phase (entire RC cycle stays GitHub-only); `2.0.0` stable will publish to npm `latest`. (6) README.md: 5-line v2 banner injected at top (don't wait for rc.4 full rewrite — would mislead anyone landing during RC cycle). Old `1.8.0-rc.3` stays in npm history (deprecate as needed).

files.change: Bump version to '2.0.0-rc.1' at root + each workspace package (cli, server, shared) using `pnpm version` or coordinated edit; old 1.8.0-rc.3 stays in npm history (deprecate later as needed)

Add new section: ## 2.0.0-rc.1 (YYYY-MM-DD). Subsections: ### BREAKING (clean rebrand from v1.x — list items: client kinds restricted, paths .fabric/rules→.fabric/knowledge, husky pre-commit gone, fabric-init skill three-piece gone). ### Removed (specific v1.x list with file paths). ### Added (v2 list — knowledge layer, dual-root, stable_id, tags field, docs/schema.md, dogfood baseline). ### Coming next (rc.2 preview: fab_extract_knowledge MCP + fabric-archive skill + Stop hooks; rc.3 preview: fab_review + fabric-review skill; rc.4 preview: doctor --lint + fabric-import + full README + docs).

Inject 5-line banner at the very top: (line 1) > **v2.0.0-rc.1 — clean rebrand to knowledge layer**. (line 2) > Status: prerelease (RC stage). (line 3) > Supported clients: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI. (line 4) > Highlights: 5-type/3-maturity/2-layer knowledge schema; dual-root (~/.fabric + <repo>/.fabric); async-review via pending/. (line 5) > Coming next: rc.2 (extract+archive), rc.3 (review), rc.4 (lint+import). Don't wait for rc.4 full README rewrite — RC-cycle landings need orientation NOW.
Create annotated tag: `git tag -a v2.0.0-rc.1 -m 'Fabric v2.0.0-rc.1 — clean rebrand to knowledge layer'`. Tag points at the release commit (final commit including all 8 tasks).
Create GitHub release via gh CLI: `gh release create v2.0.0-rc.1 --prerelease --title 'v2.0.0-rc.1' --notes-file <CHANGELOG-extracted>`. Release notes = the CHANGELOG ## 2.0.0-rc.1 section content.

implementation: Verify pre-conditions: TASK-001..007 all merged; Day-1 gate (planning-context.md §Q7) ALL grep checks pass; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint all exit 0

Bump versions: at repo root run `pnpm version 2.0.0-rc.1 --no-git-tag-version` (or workspace-aware equivalent); verify `jq -r .version package.json` returns `2.0.0-rc.1`; check workspace packages too if monorepo uses independent versioning
Write CHANGELOG.md ## 2.0.0-rc.1 section: 4 subsections (BREAKING, Removed, Added, Coming next) — content from planning-context.md §Q5 + commit messages of TASK-001..007
Inject README banner: insert 5-line block at top of README.md (above existing H1 or just under it — whichever is canonical for this README); banner should be quote-block format (> prefix)
Verify Day-1 gate fully (re-run all bash checks from planning-context.md §Q7) — must ALL pass
Stage and commit: `git add package.json */package.json CHANGELOG.md README.md && git commit -m 'chore(release): 2.0.0-rc.1'`
Create annotated tag: `git tag -a v2.0.0-rc.1 -m 'Fabric v2.0.0-rc.1 — clean rebrand to knowledge layer'`
Push commit + tag: `git push origin main && git push origin v2.0.0-rc.1`
Extract CHANGELOG ## 2.0.0-rc.1 section into a tmp file (e.g. via `awk '/## 2\.0\.0-rc\.1/,/## /' CHANGELOG.md | head -n -1` or similar)
Create GitHub prerelease: `gh release create v2.0.0-rc.1 --prerelease --title 'v2.0.0-rc.1 — clean rebrand to knowledge layer' --notes-file /tmp/v2-rc1-notes.md`
Verify GitHub release: `gh release view v2.0.0-rc.1 --json isPrerelease | jq -e '.isPrerelease == true'` returns true
Confirm NOT published to npm — npm dist-tags should still show 1.8.0-rc.3 latest (or whatever was there); 2.0.0-rc.1 should NOT appear on npm registry

reference.pattern: GitHub-only RC release (no npm during RC cycle); annotated tag + prerelease flag + 5-line README banner
reference.examples: Prior commits show ship pattern (5c4fa16 chore(release): 1.8.0-rc.1)

criteria: `gh release view v2.0.0-rc.1 --json isPrerelease | jq -e '.isPrerelease == true'` returns true
`grep -A2 '## 2.0.0-rc.1' CHANGELOG.md | grep -i breaking` returns at least 1 hit (BREAKING subsection present)
`head -10 README.md | grep -iE 'v2\.0.*rebrand|rc-stage|prerelease'` returns at least 1 hit (banner present at top)
GitHub release notes (gh release view --json body) contain BREAKING / Removed / Added / Coming next subsections
npm registry view for fabric package does NOT list 2.0.0-rc.1 as published version (verified via `npm view fabric versions` — 2.0.0-rc.1 absent)
All Day-1 gate bash checks from planning-context.md §Q7 pass

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: npm publish (deferred to 2.0.0 stable), full README rewrite (rc.4), docs/{knowledge-types,initialization,roadmap}.md (rc.4), rc.2/3/4 implementation work. Strictly: version + CHANGELOG + README banner (5 lines only) + tag + GitHub prerelease. Bug protocol: any post-tag hotfix becomes `2.0.0-rc.1.fix.N` retag, NOT a rc.2 commit.

rationale.chosen_approach: GitHub-only RC release (no npm) + 5-line README banner now (not deferred to rc.4)
alternatives_considered: Defer README banner to rc.4 full rewrite
Skip GitHub prerelease, just tag locally
decision_factors: No-npm during RC keeps `latest` clean for stable users (1.8.x → 2.0 stable jump on npm)
5-line banner is cheap and prevents misorientation for anyone landing on README during RC cycle
GitHub prerelease provides public download URL for testers without npm pollution
rationale.tradeoffs: RC adopters must `pnpm add fabric@github:<org>/fabric#v2.0.0-rc.1` instead of `pnpm add fabric@next` — slight friction, but acceptable for prerelease cohort

manual_checks: Visit GitHub release page — confirm 'Pre-release' badge visible
View README on GitHub — confirm 5-line banner is the first thing visible
Run `npm view fabric versions` — confirm 2.0.0-rc.1 NOT listed (npm clean)
Verify `git show v2.0.0-rc.1` shows annotated tag with message
success_metrics: CHANGELOG has BREAKING + Removed + Added + Coming next subsections
integration: End-to-end: clone repo at v2.0.0-rc.1 tag, run `pnpm install && pnpm build && node packages/cli/dist/index.js init` in tmp project — pipeline succeeds
